sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the bottom-right panel now shows:
- Row 1: blue, red, blue
- Row 2: red, center, red
- Row 3: blue, red, red
This is the majority pattern: b, R, b / R, center, R / b, R, R. But the level still isn't complete.
I've tried many patterns now without success. Let me think about this problem completely differently.
Maybe the goal is not about the pattern of red/blue cells at all. Maybe I need to look at the center pieces more carefully.
Looking at the bottom-right center piece:
ggggcc
ggggcc
RRWWcc
RRWWcc
ggggcc
ggggcc
The "RR" cells are on the left, not in the center. This is different from the other center pieces where "RR" is in the center.
Wait, maybe the "RR" position in the center piece indicates which cell in the 3x3 grid is the "active" cell. In the other center pieces, "RR" is at position (1,1) (center), but in the bottom-right center, "RR" is at position (1,0) (left).
If "RR" at (1,0) means the left cell is active, maybe I need to click on the left cell of the bottom-right panel?
But I've been clicking on the cells to toggle their color. Maybe I need to click on the center piece itself?
Let me try clicking on the "RR" part of the bottom-right center piece. The "RR" cells are at approximately row 46-47, col 44-45 (based on the ASCII output).
Actually, looking at the ASCII output for the bottom-right center:
ggggcc
ggggcc
RRWWcc
RRWWcc
ggggcc
ggggcc
The "RR" cells are at rows 2-3, cols 0-1 of the 6x6 center grid. In the actual board, the center is at approximately rows 44-49, cols 44-49. So the "RR" cells are at approximately rows 46-47, cols 44-45.
Let me try clicking on the "RR" part of the bottom-right center.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current bottom-right: b, R, b / R, center, R / b, R, R (majority pattern)
# Level not complete. Let me try clicking on the "RR" part of the bottom-right center.
# The "RR" cells are at approximately row 46-47, col 44-45

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 44}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
